blocks.content: He didn't plan it. He didn't plan anything. He just — moved, the way you move toward something without deciding to.
Her hand on his shoulder shifted — fingers against the back of his neck, barely, just there — and he felt it in his whole chest.

Two inches between them now. Then less. The music was still going and the lights were still low and the rest of the gym had gone distant in the way things go distant when something is happening that requires all of your attention.
Her eyes dropped — just briefly, just once — to his mouth. Came back up.
He leaned in. She leaned in. The distance between them went from inches to a breath, and the breath was the only thing left, and Kit thought: this is it, this is actually —
